skill.name: authentication-setup
skill.description: Design and implement authentication and authorization systems. Use when setting up user login, JWT tokens, OAuth, session management, or role-based access control. Handles password security, token management, SSO integration.
skill.summary: Design and implement authentication and authorization systems for applications with secure password hashing, JWT tokens, OAuth integration, and role-based access control.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This is an educational documentation skill that provides guidance on implementing authentication systems. All static findings are false positives. The skill teaches best practices including bcrypt/argon2 for password hashing, environment variable management for secrets, proper JWT token handling, and legitimate command-line tools for secret generation. No malicious behavior detected.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers implement secure authentication and authorization for applications, covering JWT tokens, OAuth integration, password hashing, and role-based access control with battle-tested patterns.
actual_capabilities: Design database schemas for users and authentication with proper password hashing using bcrypt or argon2
Implement JWT token generation and verification with access and refresh token flows
Create authentication middleware for protecting API endpoints with role-based authorization
Set up OAuth 2.0 integration with social providers like Google and GitHub
Build registration, login, token refresh, and logout API endpoints
Configure proper environment variable management for sensitive secrets
limitations: Does not provide pre-built authentication libraries or frameworks - only guides implementation
Does not include frontend authentication UI components
Does not cover biometric authentication or hardware token integration
Does not include built-in rate limiting or DDoS protection - must be added separately

use_cases.title: New Application Authentication
use_cases.description: Add secure user authentication to a new Express.js or FastAPI application with JWT tokens and password hashing
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building new web applications

use_cases.title: API Security Layer
use_cases.description: Protect existing REST or GraphQL APIs with authentication middleware and role-based access control

use_cases.title: Social Login Integration
use_cases.description: Add OAuth 2.0 social login with Google, GitHub, or other providers to enable SSO

prompt_templates.title: Basic JWT Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up JWT authentication for my Express.js app with PostgreSQL. Include password hashing with bcrypt, access tokens with 15-minute expiry, and refresh tokens with 7-day expiry.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants basic JWT authentication for a Node.js application

prompt_templates.title: Role-Based Access Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Add role-based access control to my API. Create admin, editor, and user roles. Protect delete endpoints so only admins can access them.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs permission levels beyond simple authentication

prompt_templates.title: OAuth Social Login
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Google and GitHub OAuth login to my application. Users should be able to register and login using their social accounts.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to add social authentication providers

prompt_templates.title: Complete Auth System
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete authentication system with registration, login, logout, token refresh, email verification, and optional MFA using Google Authenticator.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a full-featured authentication implementation

output_examples.input: Set up JWT authentication with Express.js and PostgreSQL
output_examples.output: The skill provides a complete project structure with src/auth/ directory containing password.ts for hashing, jwt.ts for token management, middleware.ts for route protection, and routes.ts for auth endpoints. Includes database schema SQL, environment variable templates, and step-by-step implementation instructions.

output_examples.input: Add role-based authorization to my API
output_examples.output: The skill generates requireRole middleware that checks user roles, example protected routes for admin-only endpoints, and integration with authenticateToken for combined auth + authorization.

best_practices: Always use proven hashing algorithms like bcrypt or argon2 with salt rounds of 10 or higher - never plaintext or weak hashes
Store secrets in environment variables, never hardcode in source code, and add .env files to .gitignore
Use short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) with longer-lived refresh tokens (7-30 days) for optimal security and user experience
anti_patterns: Storing passwords in plaintext or using weak hashing algorithms like MD5 or SHA1
Hardcoding JWT secrets or API keys directly in source code
Including sensitive data like passwords or credit card numbers in JWT token payloads

faq.question: What password hashing algorithm should I use?
faq.answer: Use bcrypt for Node.js applications or argon2 for Python applications. Both are proven algorithms with adjustable work factors. Set salt rounds to 10 or higher for adequate security.

faq.question: How long should JWT tokens live?
faq.answer: Access tokens should be short-lived (15 minutes) for quick revocation on security events. Refresh tokens can be longer (7-30 days) but should be stored in a database for revocation capability.

faq.question: Can I store user passwords in JWT tokens?
faq.answer: No. JWT tokens are encoded but not encrypted, meaning anyone can decode them. Only include non-sensitive data like user_id and role in token payloads.

faq.question: How do I handle token refresh securely?
faq.answer: Store refresh tokens in your database with expiry times. When a user logs out or security is compromised, delete the token from the database to prevent reuse.

faq.question: Should I use sessions or JWT?
faq.answer: JWT is preferred for stateless APIs and mobile applications. Sessions work well for traditional server-rendered applications. JWT provides better scalability and works across domains.

faq.question: How do I implement OAuth correctly?
faq.answer: Use established libraries like Passport.js or NextAuth. Never store OAuth tokens in localStorage - use secure HTTP-only cookies. Validate state parameters to prevent CSRF attacks.
